---
title: "Getting Started"
sidebar: en
---

## Requirements

- A self-hosted, reachable Nextcloud instance (HTTPS recommended).
- An **app password** for your user (see below).

## Create an app password in Nextcloud

App passwords replace the main password and can be revoked per device.

1. Sign in to Nextcloud.
2. Avatar in the top right → **Personal Settings** → **Security**.
3. Under **Devices & sessions**: enter a name (e.g. "Etabli Nuage — iPhone") and click **Create new app password**.
4. Note the URL (`https://cloud.example.org`) and the displayed app password — the latter is shown only once.

## Configure the app

1. Install the app from the store and open it.
2. In the setup dialog, enter:
   - **Server URL**
   - **Username**
   - **App password**
3. The sign-in covers all four modules — one account, one configuration.

![After a successful sign-in: module overview.](../_screens/android-main.png){width=320}

## Module status

| Module | State |
|--------|-------|
| **Files (WebDAV)** | round-trip ready: browse, download, upload, rename, delete. |
| **Link Checker** | round-trip ready: check a CSV of URLs, write the status log back to Nextcloud. |
| **Contacts (CardDAV)** | in development — not yet for production use. |
| **Calendar (CalDAV)** | in development — not yet for production use. |

## Notes

- App passwords can be revoked at any time in Nextcloud under **Security → Devices & sessions** — essential if a device is lost.
- Self-signed certificates are not accepted automatically.
- The app only contacts the configured Nextcloud URL.
